title: Descriptive: human fixed substitutions show ~2:1 transition/transversion ratio, ~3.4-fold enriched over the unselected germline mutational input

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Descriptive: human fixed substitutions show ~2:1 transition/transversion ratio, '
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

statistics.name: titv_enrichment_fixed_over_input
statistics.note: Derived: s_titv_fixed / s_titv_input. Selection enriches transitions ~3.4x over unselected input.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts: (1) absolute Ti/Tv > 1.5 establishes a transition-biased fixation regime (the literature consensus value is ~2:1 in human; threshold 1.5 is the lower bound that still licenses the descriptive claim); (2) fixed Ti/Tv > input Ti/Tv establishes that the bias is selection-driven, not an artifact of the mutational input. The descriptive claim is the empirical premise on which dual_channel_selection_hypothesis depends; encoded separately so the descriptive fact and the mechanistic hypothesis are distinct nodes in the claim graph.
inference.failure_mode: If s_titv_fixed approached s_titv_input (ratio near 1.0), the bias would dissolve into the mutational input and the dual_channel_selection_hypothesis would lose its empirical anchor.

conclusion.assertion: Human lineage-specific fixed substitutions show a Ti/Tv ratio of ~2.05, against an unselected germline mutational input of ~0.6 (Kong et al. 2012 deCODE family trios). Selection enriches transitions ~3.4-fold over the unselected expectation. This is the descriptive empirical anchor for the dual-channel selection hypothesis (sibling claim).

scope.scale_note: Genome-wide Ti/Tv. Per-region (CDS / intron / UTR) decompositions and the 4-fold-degenerate-codon-position decomposition are sibling claims.

external_assumptions.statement: Kong 2012 deCODE family-trio mutational input estimate (Ti/Tv ~ 0.6) is a defensible upper bound for the unselected human germline; alternative estimates (1KG de novo, gnomAD low-frequency) shift the enrichment ratio modestly but not its sign.
